tool_args.label: Flatbush Ave Bus Priority - Community Board 6 Presentation

payload.title: Flatbush Ave Bus Priority
payload.description: Presented to Community Board 6 Transportation & Public Safety Committee on June 27, 2024

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Flatbush Avenue
tool_args.raw_text: Flatbush Ave is a major corridor in Brooklyn with connections to major job centers, housing, entertainment, culture, shopping, and recreation.

payload.description: Critical transportation corridor in Brooklyn with connections to major job centers, housing, entertainment, culture, shopping, and recreation. Vision Zero Priority Corridor. Connects to Atlantic Terminal (LIRR, 9 subway lines), 2/5 trains at Nostrand Junction, B/Q/S trains at Prospect Park.

tool_args.label: Flatbush Avenue Bus Priority
tool_args.raw_text: Current round of design options will focus on Downtown Brooklyn/ Northern Flatbush section of the corridor

payload.description: Current round of design options focuses on Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush corridor section. Three design options being studied: curbside bus lanes, offset bus lanes, and center-running bus lanes. DOT plans to study bus priority treatments in other sections in future phases.

tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

warnings: entity.parent_organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Parent organizations are relation context. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

tool_args.label: Brooklyn Community Board 6 Transportation & Public Safety Committee

payload.entity_name: Brooklyn Community Board 6 Transportation & Public Safety Committee

payload.description: Primary route on Flatbush Ave operating local and limited service from Downtown Brooklyn to Kings Plaza/Bergen Beach.
Operates on Flatbush Ave; planned Bus Only Signal at Parkside Ave for B41 and B12.

tool_args.label: Bus Only Signals on Flatbush Ave

payload.description: Queue Jump Signals enable buses to get a head start to bypass traffic, often paired with a Leading Pedestrian Interval. 3 Bus Only Signals planned for Summer 2024 installation on Flatbush Ave.
locations: Ocean Ave/Empire Blvd for B41 (northbound and southbound)
Parkside Ave for B41 and B12 (northbound, southbound and eastbound)
Church Ave for B41 (northbound and southbound)

locations_normalized.raw_text: Ocean Ave/Empire Blvd for B41 (northbound and southbound)
Parkside Ave for B41 and B12 (northbound, southbound and eastbound)
Church Ave for B41 (northbound and southbound)

tool_args.label: Transit Signal Priority on Flatbush Ave

payload.description: In 2022, NYC DOT installed Transit Signal Priority (TSP) at 37 intersections on Flatbush Ave. TSP modifies traffic signal timing by providing additional green time for buses when they are near an intersection.

tool_args.label: Community Board 6 Transportation & Public Safety Committee Meeting

payload.description: Flatbush Ave Bus Priority presentation to Community Board 6 Transportation & Public Safety Committee
Community Advisory Board meeting #2. Community Boards 2, 6, 8.

warnings: event.organizers is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organizer lists refer to entity records. Suggested relation: organized_by.

tool_args.label: Summer 2024 On-Street Surveys and Traffic Analysis

payload.description: On-street surveys for merchants and bus riders. Continue traffic analysis and project design.
Present results of traffic analysis and preferred design to Community Advisory Board, Community Boards, and additional stakeholders.
Continue to refine design based on traffic analysis and public feedback.
Proposed implementation of bus priority treatments.

payload.claim_text: Over 132,000 average daily bus riders along 12 MTA bus routes use Flatbush Ave. B41 alone serves over 28,000 average daily riders. Slow bus speeds (below 4 mph in Downtown Brooklyn).
payload.subject: Flatbush Ave bus performance

payload.claim_text: Flatbush Ave is a Vision Zero (VZ) Priority Corridor with 11 VZ Priority Intersections, is located within a VZ Priority Zone and intersects with 18 other VZ Priority Corridors. Downtown section ranks within the top 10% of high crash corridors in Brooklyn, with 55 people killed or severely injured since 2019. Flatbush Ave is located within a Priority Youth Injury Area.
payload.subject: Flatbush Ave safety

payload.claim_text: CBs 2, 6, 8, 9, 14, 17, 18. 985,000 residents. 59% of households have no access to a private vehicle. 76% commute to work via public transit, walking, or biking. Citywide, bus rider median annual income is lower than drivers ($30,000 vs. $47,000). 69% of residents are non-White. 44% Black, 12% Hispanic, 7% Asian, 7% Other. Multiple Senior and Youth Injury Areas.
payload.subject: Flatbush Ave corridor demographics

payload.claim_text: Current round of design options will focus on Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush section of the corridor. 69,000 average daily bus riders on 6 MTA bus routes use this section of Flatbush Ave. DOT plans to study bus priority treatments in other sections of the corridor in future phases.
payload.subject: Project phasing

tool_args.label: Bus Speeds Between Atlantic Av and Grand Army Plaza

payload.claim_text: Bus speeds between Atlantic Av and Grand Army Plaza are very slow throughout the entire day. Below 5 mph most of the day northbound and during PM peak southbound. Average non-express weekday peak bus speed in Brooklyn 6.3 mph. Traffic congestion and double-parking cause slowdowns, especially in commercial areas.
